event: fetal distress was noted on the cardiotocography
decision was made to proceed to a ventouse-assisted delivery
required immediate resuscitation with mask ventilation
transferred to the neonatal intensive care unit (NICU)
supported with synchronized intermittent positive pressure ventilation (SIPPV) with minimal pressures
no antibiotics were given to the mother
blood cultures taken from the newborn pre–antibiotic treatment
empirical antibiotics were commenced for presumed early-onset sepsis
initial full blood counts showed neutropenia with significant left shift
mother remained completely well after birth
N. meningitidis genogroup W DNA detected by real-time PCR
negative for a range of pathogens including meningococcus
light pure growth of an organism
MALDI-TOF mass spectrometry identifying Neisseria meningitidis
clinically, the baby continued to improve
benzylpenicillin dosage was decreased back to 60 mg/kg 12-hourly
real-time PCR assay targeting N. meningitidis ctrA and porA genes performed
real-time PCR assay targeting genogroups B, C, W, and Y performed
confirmed the detection of a meningococcus genogroup W clonal complex 11 (CC11) organism
suspected to be caused by maternally acquired Neisseria meningitidis serogroup W clonal complex 11
contact tracing identified 14 staff members, the 2 parents, and 3 grandparents to be at risk
vaccination with quadrivalent conjugate meningococcal vaccine provided
complete a 5-day course of β-lactam therapy
discharged home from the NICU on day 8 of life
